chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يَسْتَتِرُ بِهِ الْمُصَلِّي مِمَّنْ يَمُرُّ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 14 – What the praying one can veil with from the ones who are passing in front of him

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/69)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/69)
text: كَانَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَجْعَلُ الْعَنَزَةَ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ إِذَا صَلَّى .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from MUawiya Bin Wahab,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww used to place the walking stick in front of himsaww when hesaww prayed Salāt’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from Mu‘awiyah ibn Wahab who has said the following: “Abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘The Messenger of Allah would place his staff in front of the place where he performed Salah (prayer).’”
